MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR MONDAY, JUNE 7, 2010. PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH FESTINESE AND MONIQUE MODESTE FESTINESE, INNOCENTE CASSOLATO, TRUSTEE OF THE INNOCENTE AND CAROL CASSOLATO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORY AND INSPECTION DEMANDS IS GRANTED. AS TO INTERROGATORY NO. 3, THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 3. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 3 AS THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND ORDERED STRICKEN. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND FURTHER AFTER DILIGENT SEARCH AND INQUIRY AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2031.230. THE COURT FINDS THE RESPONSES AND OBJECTIONS WERE MADE WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $790, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT HEWLETT JR., NOT REPORTED (610).

NOTICE OF UNAVAILABILITY OF LEVY, RAYMOND HERBERT ON JUN-22-2010 THROUGH JUN-22-2010 JUN 24 AND JUN 28, JUL 06, JUL 11 TO JUL 20, JUL 27, AUG 06, SEP 10 TO SEP 17, SEP 20, NOV 01, AND NOV 15, 2010 FILED BY PLAINTIFF JOSEPH FESTINESE AND MONIQUE MODESTE FESTINESE TRUSTEES OF THE JOSEPH AND MONIQUE FESTINESE FAMILY TRUST INNOCENTE CASSOLATO, TRUSTEE OF THE INNOCENTE CASSOLATO 1991 LIVING TRUST CASSOLATO, CAROL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH FESTINESE AND MONIQUE MODESTE FESTINESE, INNOCENTE CASSOLATO (AS TRUSTEES) AND INNOCENTE CASSOLATO'S (AS TRUSTEE) MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED IN PART. MOTION IMPROPERLY SEEKS TO ADJUDICATE SEPARATE ISSUES OF FACT AND DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF A CAUSE OF ACTION, AN AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, A CLAIM FOR DAMAGES, OR AN ISSUE OF DUTY. (SEE CCP SECTION 437C(F)(1).) THE NOTICE OF MOTION DOES NOT STATE THE SPECIFIC CAUSE OF ACTION, AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, CLAIMS FOR DAMAGES, OR ISSUES OF DUTY, AND REPEAT THEM, VERBATIM, IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS. (SEE CRC 3.1350(B). MOTION IS GRANTED, HOWEVER, AS TO INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT AND COURT ORDERS PARTITION PURSUANT TO AGREEMENT OF PARTIES. THE PROCEEDS WILL BE HELD IN ESCROW PENDING DETERMINATION OF THE PARTIES' INTERESTS. THE COURT DENIES WITHOUT PREJUDICE, THE REQUEST TO APPOINT REFEREE WHICH MAY BE THE SUBJECT OF FUTURE MOTION. MR. MEREDITH TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-04-2011 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Set For Case Management Conference On Feb-18-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH FESTINESE AND MONIQUE MODESTE FESTINESE, INNOCENTE CASSOLATO (as trustees) AND INNOCENTE CASSOLATO'S (as trustee) MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Roger L. Meredith (616.5959) for defendant and responding party; Raymond H. Levy (753.8080) for plaintiff and moving party. Discussed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: summary adjudication denied in part. Motion improperly seeks to adjudicate separate issues of fact and does not completely dispose of a cause of action, an affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty. (see CCP section 437c(f)(1).) The notice of motion does not state the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty, and repeat them, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts. (see CRC 3.1350(b). Motion is granted, however, as to interlocutory judgment and court orders partition pursuant to agreement of parties. The proceeds will be held in escrow pending determination of the parties' interests. The court denies without prejudice, the request to appoint referee which may be the subject of future motion. Mr. Meredith to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatory And Inspection Demands, Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 7, 2010. No appearance by either party. PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH FESTINESE AND MONIQUE MODESTE FESTINESE, INNOCENTE CASSOLATO, TRUSTEE OF THE INNOCENTE AND CAROL CASSOLATO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To interrogatory And Inspection Demands is granted. As to Interrogatory No. 3, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further. The motion is granted as to Request For Production No. 3 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further after diligent search and inquiry and in compliance with the Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2031.230. The court finds the responses and objections were made without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $790, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.

Notice Of Motion For Interlocutory Decree For Appointment Of Referee And Order For Sale Of Realty
Off Calendar Mar-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH FESTINESE AND MONIQUE MODESTE FESTINESE, Trustees of the Joseph and Monique Festinese Family Trust, and INNOCENTE CASSOLATO, Trustee of the Innocente Cassolato 1991 Living Trust. Motion for Interlocutory Decree for Appointment of Referee and Order for Sale of Realty. Appearances: Raymond H. Levy, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 753-8080 appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Roger Louis Meredith, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 616-5959 appearing for Defendant Jean Judnick. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. CCP section 872.710(a) provides at trial the court shall determine whether the plaintiff has a right to partition. This motion is premature. No competent evidence establishes the parties interests. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
